---
title: "ProSIFT -- Handoff File"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

# ProSIFT -- Handoff File

**Last Updated:** 2026-03-30 (cleaned up: session logs 2026-03-20 through 2026-03-27 archived, resolved questions archived, completed tasks archived, all promoted to Project Documentation)

**Purpose:** Operational coordination across Claude sessions. Tracks current development state, active tasks, blockers, and environment details. Updated every session.

**Key principle:** The Project Documentation (ProSIFT_project_documentation.Rmd) is the architectural reference. This handoff is purely operational -- it tracks *what is happening now*, not *why things are designed the way they are*. Reference the Project Documentation for explanations rather than restating them.

---

### DOCUMENT MAINTENANCE RULES:
- **ADD** new information to appropriate sections; do not remove existing content unless archiving
- **EDIT** only when specific information needs correction
- **ARCHIVE** session log entries older than 4-6 weeks once their content has been captured in the Project Documentation
- **REFERENCE** the Project Documentation for design rationale (format: [See Project Documentation Section X.X: Title])

---

# 1. Instance Instructions

## 1.1 Instructions for Claude Code

### Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read the Project Documentation (if instructed) and this Handoff File
- Check "Current Development State" (Section 2) for module status overview
- Check "Pending Tasks" (Section 4.1) for assigned work items
- Review the File System Map (Section 7) to orient yourself to the project structure
- Check Computational Environment (Section 8) for environment setup
- Review Code and Style Preferences (Section 9) before writing any code

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- Follow the QC check protocol below -- pause between major steps, report metrics, and ask before proceeding
- When writing scripts, use the header format and style conventions from Section 9
- When referencing architectural decisions or design rationale, check the Project Documentation or ask the user to provide it if deeper context is needed
- When implementing cross-module interfaces, check "Integration Points" (Section 6) for data contracts
- If a module's expected inputs or outputs change during implementation, update Section 6 immediately

**AT SESSION END:**

- Update Session Log (Section 3) with a dated entry of work done, including:
  - Scripts created or modified (with file paths)
  - Output files generated (with file paths)
  - Tests run and their outcomes
  - Any warnings, unexpected results, or concerns
- Update "Current Development State" (Section 2) if module statuses changed
- Move completed tasks to "Completed Tasks" (Section 4.3) with date
- Update File System Map (Section 7) if files were created, moved, or deleted
- Flag any issues or questions in Section 5
- If a significant design decision was made, note it here and flag it for promotion to the Project Documentation Decision Register

### QC Check Protocol (Mandatory)

- Pause between major implementation steps to verify outputs before proceeding
- Report QC metrics after each step -- do not continue silently
- Ask the user if results look reasonable before moving to the next part
- If any test fails or produces unexpected results, STOP and report

**Check-in format after major steps:**
```
Step [X] complete.
  Results:
  - Output file: [path]
  - [Key metric 1]: [value] (expected: [range])
  - [Key metric 2]: [value] (expected: [range])
  QC Status: [PASS / WARNING / FAIL]
  [Notes or concerns]
  Ready to proceed to Step [Y]. Continue? (y/n)
```

**Red flags to watch for and report:**

- Tests that pass when they should fail (or vice versa)
- Unexpected file sizes, row counts, or data dimensions
- Schema mismatches between modules (check Section 6)
- Warnings from statistical or data processing functions
- Discrepancies between what was specified and what the data supports
- Silent data loss (rows/columns dropped without explanation)

---

## 1.2 Instructions for Claude.ai

### Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read the Project Documentation (if instructed) and this Handoff File
- Review recent Session Log entries (Section 3) to understand what has been done since your last session
- Check "Open Questions" (Section 5) for items that may need attention

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- When making significant design or architectural decisions, document them using the Decision Register format in Project Documentation Section 9
- When drafting implementation tasks, write them as actionable entries with clear acceptance criteria for the Pending Tasks queue (Section 4.1)
- When reviewing code or implementation approaches, cross-reference with the Project Documentation for architectural consistency

**AT SESSION END:**

- Promote Session Log content to the Project Documentation as appropriate:
  - Significant decisions go to Project Documentation Section 9 (Decision Register)
  - Development milestones go to Project Documentation Section 10 (Development Log)
  - New limitations discovered go to Project Documentation Section 8
- Add any new tasks to "Pending Tasks" (Section 4.1)
- Flag any open questions or blockers in Section 5

### Role and Responsibilities

**Primary responsibilities:**

- Architectural decisions and design review
- Evaluating implementation approaches and tradeoffs
- Reviewing code logic and analytical methodology
- Drafting task specifications with clear acceptance criteria
- Literature review and biological context for module design (e.g., imputation method selection, enrichment analysis design)

**Quality and validation mindset:**

- Question whether implementation choices match the architectural intent in the Project Documentation
- Consider edge cases: what happens with missing data, many-to-many ID mappings, API failures
- Verify that module interfaces match the data contracts in Section 6
- Be critical of Claude Code's outputs -- check whether the approach was sound, not just whether the code ran
- Flag scope creep: is this implementation adding complexity beyond what was specified?

---

# 2. Current Development State

## 2.1 Phase Overview

| Phase | Description | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Core pipeline skeleton + pre-normalization QC | Complete | All Module 01 processes + Module 02 implemented and tested |
| Phase 2 | Normalization, imputation, statistical analysis, enrichment + QC report assembly | In progress | Modules 03 and 04 complete. Module 05 (enrichment) next. |
| Phase 3 | Database integration layer | Not started | [See Project Documentation Section 6.3] |
| Phase 4 | Interactive frontend | Not started | [See Project Documentation Section 6.4] |

**Current phase:** Phase 2 in progress.

**Current focus:** Module 04 (Differential Abundance) complete (2026-03-30). Module 05 (ORA/GSEA Enrichment) design and implementation next.

## 2.2 Module Status

Tracks implementation status of individual modules within and across phases. A module may be: Not started, In progress, Functional (works but incomplete), Complete, or Blocked.

| Module | Phase | Status | Last Touched | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Project structure / repo setup | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | main.nf, nextflow.config, conf/base.config, workflows/, modules/local/, bin/ |
| nextflow.config + params.yml schema | 1 | Functional | 2026-03-26 | local/pbs/conda/singularity profiles; cluster path needs updating before first cluster run |
| Conda environment | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-25 | environment.yml finalized; prosift env exists locally; not yet created on cluster |
| Data preparation scripts | Pre-1 | Complete | 2026-03-26 | 3 scripts in scripts/; samplesheet.csv generated; 12 per-run input directories |
| Module 01: Input Validation & ID Mapping | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-27 | All processes (4.1-4.10) implemented and tested. 5 scripts in bin/. |
| Module 02: Pre-normalization QC/EDA | 1 | Complete | 2026-03-27 | bin/prenorm_qc.py; 17 outputs; CTXcyto benchmark verified |
| Module 03: Normalization & Imputation | 2 | Complete | 2026-03-30 | NORMALIZE + IMPUTE both implemented and tested. Full pipeline test all 12 runs, 0 NaN remaining. |
| QC Report Assembly | 2 | Not started | -- | Collects diagnostics from Modules 02-03 into consolidated HTML report |
| Module 04: Differential Abundance (DEqMS) | 2 | Complete | 2026-03-30 | bin/differential_abundance.py; limma+DEqMS via rpy2; 7 outputs; tested all 12 runs. |
| Module 05: ORA / GSEA enrichment | 2 | Not started | -- | -- |
| Module 06: Database queries (all 5 databases) | 3 | Not started | -- | -- |
| Module 07: Results assembly (SQLite) | 3 | Not started | -- | -- |
| Module 08: Interactive frontend | 4 | Not started | -- | Tech selection deferred |
| Singularity definition | 1 | Not started | -- | Dependency list established; ready to write .def file |

## 2.3 Nextflow Process Status

Tracks which Nextflow processes exist and whether they are functional. Populated as processes are implemented.

| Process Name | Module | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| VALIDATE_INPUTS | 01 | Functional | bin/validate_inputs.py; tested all 12 runs |
| FILTER_PROTEINS | 01 | Functional | bin/filter_proteins.py; tested all 12 runs |
| UNIPROT_MAPPING | 01 | Functional | bin/uniprot_mapping.py; paginated JSON API; MD5 cache; tested all 12 runs |
| MISSINGNESS_REPORT | 01 | Functional | bin/missingness_report.py; errorStrategy 'ignore'; tested all 12 runs |
| PRENORM_QC | 02 | Functional | bin/prenorm_qc.py; 17 outputs; tested all 12 runs |
| NORMALIZE | 03 | Functional | bin/normalize.py; 12 emit channels; tested all 12 runs (2026-03-30) |
| IMPUTE | 03 | Functional | bin/impute.py; 8 emit channels; tested all 12 runs (2026-03-30) |
| DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE | 04 | Functional | bin/differential_abundance.py; limma + DEqMS via rpy2; volcano + MA plots; 7 named emit channels; tested all 12 runs (2026-03-30) |

---

# 3. Session Log

Maintained by Claude Code. Claude.ai reads these entries to understand what work has been done and promotes substantive content to the Project Documentation (decisions to Section 9, milestones to Section 10).

> **Entry template:**
>
> ### [DATE] ([Claude Code / Claude.ai])
> - [What was done]
> - [Key outputs generated with file paths]
> - [Tests run and outcomes]
> - [Decisions made -- flag for promotion to Project Documentation Decision Register if significant]
> - [Issues encountered or flagged]

### 2026-03-30 (Claude Code) -- Module 04 Differential Abundance Implemented, Tested, and Wired

- **bin/differential_abundance.py implemented (Module 04 DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE process):**
  - Contrast parsing + validation: `numerator_vs_denominator` format, split on first `_vs_`, validated against metadata group column before any R calls.
  - Peptide count summarization: `min of nonzero` per-protein across all samples (zeros = imputed positions are excluded from min). Produces `n_peptides` vector for DEqMS.
  - rpy2 bridge: Data passed to R via `ro.globalenv["prosift_*"]` variables. Linear model chain: `lmFit` → `contrasts.fit` → `eBayes` → `spectraCounteBayes`. R code executed as a string block. Results converted back using `localconverter(ro.default_converter + pandas2ri.converter)` context manager (rpy2 3.5+ API -- `pandas2ri.activate()` is deprecated).
  - Output schema: 14 columns -- protein_id, gene_symbol, log2_fc, avg_abundance, limma_t, limma_pvalue, limma_adj_pvalue, deqms_t, deqms_pvalue, deqms_adj_pvalue, n_peptides, significant, direction, contrast.
  - id_mapping join: Uses `input_id` column (not `protein_id`) as the protein identifier key in the id_mapping parquet. `id_col = "input_id" if "input_id" in id_mapping_df.columns else "protein_id"`.
  - Volcano plot: -log10(DEqMS adj.P) vs. log2FC; colored by direction (up=#d62728, down=#1f77b4, NS=#cccccc); threshold lines at FC cutoff and adj.P cutoff; produced per contrast as PNG + HTML.
  - MA plot: log2FC vs. avg_abundance; same coloring + thresholds; per contrast as PNG + HTML.
  - Summary text: method, contrast, significance threshold, significant counts by direction, top 10 hits by DEqMS adj.P.
  - Outputs: `.diff_abundance_results.parquet`, `.diff_abundance_results.csv`, `.diff_abundance_summary.txt`, `{contrast}.volcano_plot.png/.html`, `{contrast}.ma_plot.png/.html` (contrast in filename for consistent glob patterns across single/multi-contrast runs).

- **modules/local/differential_abundance/main.nf created:**
  - 7 named emit channels: results_table, results_table_csv, summary_txt, volcano_png, volcano_html, ma_png, ma_html.
  - Publishes to `{run_id}/differential_abundance/`.
  - Stub block dynamically extracts contrast name from params.yml at runtime using `grep -A 20 'contrasts:' | grep -m1 '^[[:space:]]*-' | sed | tr -d '"'`, avoiding hardcoded contrast names.

- **workflows/prosift.nf updated:**
  - `ch_input` multiMap expanded to 7 branches: `da_params` added as 7th branch.
  - DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE wired: `IMPUTE.out.imputed_matrix.join(VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.metadata).join(UNIPROT_MAPPING.out.mapping_table).join(ch_input.da_params)` → `ch_da_input` → `DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE(ch_da_input)`.
  - Module import added.

- **nextflow.config updated (conda profile):**
  - Added `R_HOME` and `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH` to `process.beforeScript` so rpy2 can locate libR.dylib on macOS. Without `R_HOME`, rpy2 segfaults (exit code 139) on macOS even when R is on PATH.

- **conf/base.config updated:**
  - Added `maxForks = 2` for DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE (and already applied to PRENORM_QC, NORMALIZE, IMPUTE, MISSINGNESS_REPORT) to cap concurrent Chrome instances from kaleido. Running 12 processes in parallel caused Chrome to hang on macOS.

- **Standalone test on CTXcyto benchmark:**
  - 8,403 proteins, contrast KO_vs_WT. 778 significant (9.3%): 395 up, 383 down. Top hit: sp|Q9WV60|.
  - All 7 output files produced. Volcano and MA plots confirmed functional.

- **Bugs found and fixed during development:**
  1. `pandas2ri.activate()` DeprecationWarning (rpy2 3.5+): removed activate(), switched to `localconverter` context manager.
  2. rpy2 segfault (exit 139): R_HOME + DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH not set. Fixed in nextflow.config conda beforeScript and verified with standalone test.
  3. `KeyError: protein_id` in id_mapping join: id_mapping parquet uses `input_id` not `protein_id` as protein key. Fixed with conditional column detection.
  4. `n_peptides` dtype int32 (R returns int32; schema requires int64): fixed with `.astype("Int64")` (pandas nullable Int64).
  5. Stub block hardcoded contrast name `KO_vs_WT`: fraction/region runs produced stale empty stub files with wrong contrast in filename. Fixed by dynamically extracting contrast from params.yml in the stub shell block. Stale files removed manually from results.
  6. kaleido/Chrome hang at 11/12 runs: too many parallel Chrome instances on macOS. Fixed with `maxForks = 2` in conf/base.config.

- **Full 12-run pipeline test (all modules, Modules 01-04):**
  - All 12 runs completed successfully. Contrasts correct per run type.
  - Significance counts biologically sensible: genotype comparisons 0.4-9.3% significant; fraction comparisons ~50%; region comparisons ~5%.

  | Run | Significant | Up | Down | % Significant |
  |---|---|---|---|---|
  | CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO | 778 / 8403 | 395 | 383 | 9.3% |
  | CTXsynap_WT_vs_CTXsynap_KO | 33 / 8287 | 4 | 29 | 0.4% |
  | HIPcyto_WT_vs_HIPcyto_KO | 192 / 8004 | 23 | 169 | 2.4% |
  | HIPsynap_WT_vs_HIPsynap_KO | 43 / 8252 | 13 | 30 | 0.5% |
  | CTXcyto_KO_vs_CTXsynap_KO | ~50% | -- | -- | synap-specific proteins |
  | CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXsynap_WT | ~50% | -- | -- | synap-specific proteins |
  | CTXcyto_KO_vs_HIPcyto_KO | ~5% | -- | -- | regional comparison |
  | CTXcyto_WT_vs_HIPcyto_WT | ~5% | -- | -- | regional comparison |
  | CTXsynap_KO_vs_HIPsynap_KO | ~5% | -- | -- | regional comparison |
  | CTXsynap_WT_vs_HIPsynap_WT | ~5% | -- | -- | regional comparison |
  | HIPcyto_KO_vs_HIPsynap_KO | ~50% | -- | -- | fraction comparison |
  | HIPcyto_WT_vs_HIPsynap_WT | ~50% | -- | -- | fraction comparison |

### 2026-03-30 (Claude Code) -- Full Pipeline Test (Modules 01-03), Bug Fix in impute.py

- **Full end-to-end Nextflow pipeline run on all 12 CTX/Synaptosome runs:**
  - Command: `nextflow run main.nf -profile local,conda --samplesheet prosift_inputs/samplesheet.csv --outdir results_fulltest`
  - All 7 processes (VALIDATE_INPUTS, FILTER_PROTEINS, UNIPROT_MAPPING, MISSINGNESS_REPORT, PRENORM_QC, NORMALIZE, IMPUTE) ran successfully on all 12 runs.
  - 44 output files per run, consistent across all 12. 0 NaN remaining in any imputed matrix.

- **Bug discovered and fixed: execute bit missing on normalize.py, impute.py, prosift_plot_utils.py:**
  - First run attempt failed with `Permission denied` on `normalize.py`. The `Write` tool creates files without the execute bit set. Nextflow calls scripts directly as executables (equivalent to `./normalize.py`), which requires the execute bit. Running scripts with `python bin/normalize.py` (as done in standalone tests) bypasses this requirement, so the issue was invisible until the full pipeline run.
  - Fixed with `chmod +x bin/*.py`. All 8 bin/ scripts now executable.
  - [FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]: Future scripts added to `bin/` must have `chmod +x` applied immediately after creation.

- **Bug discovered and fixed: impute.py `.txt` summary overcounted MAR proteins:**
  - The `write_imputation_summary_txt` function (and logging block) counted MAR proteins as `(n_mar_imputed > 0).sum()`. This overcounts by including SINGLE-GROUP (MNAR-classified) proteins that also have sporadic NaN in their detected group -- those NaN receive KNN imputation, so `n_mar_imputed > 0`, but the protein's `imputation_class` is "MNAR".
  - For CTXcyto: reported 519 MAR proteins in the txt, but parquet correctly showed 461. Difference: 58 SINGLE-GROUP proteins with 1 sporadic NaN in detected group (3 MinProb + 1 KNN each).
  - Fixed: both locations now use `(imputation_summary["imputation_class"] == "MNAR/MAR").sum()`, matching the parquet classification.
  - The imputed value counts (MNAR values, MAR values, total) were always correct -- only the protein-level counts in the txt were wrong.

- **Full pipeline results across all 12 runs (median normalization, mixed imputation):**

| Run | Proteins | MNAR proteins | MAR proteins | MNAR values | MAR values | Total imputed |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO | 8,403 | 124 | 461 | 372 | 586 | 958 |
| CTXsynap_WT_vs_CTXsynap_KO | 8,287 | 151 | 435 | 453 | 583 | 1,036 |
| HIPcyto_WT_vs_HIPcyto_KO | 8,004 | 103 | 774 | 309 | 1,018 | 1,327 |
| HIPsynap_WT_vs_HIPsynap_KO | 8,252 | 95 | 443 | 285 | 602 | 887 |
| CTXcyto_KO_vs_CTXsynap_KO | 8,421 | 406 | 489 | 1,218 | 618 | 1,836 |
| CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXsynap_WT | 8,418 | 480 | 551 | 1,440 | 667 | 2,107 |
| CTXcyto_KO_vs_HIPcyto_KO | 8,247 | 235 | 518 | 705 | 727 | 1,432 |
| CTXcyto_WT_vs_HIPcyto_WT | 8,250 | 249 | 738 | 747 | 957 | 1,704 |
| CTXsynap_KO_vs_HIPsynap_KO | 8,285 | 171 | 424 | 513 | 578 | 1,091 |
| CTXsynap_WT_vs_HIPsynap_WT | 8,294 | 141 | 485 | 423 | 632 | 1,055 |
| HIPcyto_KO_vs_HIPsynap_KO | 8,236 | 569 | 597 | 1,707 | 787 | 2,494 |
| HIPcyto_WT_vs_HIPsynap_WT | 8,321 | 633 | 840 | 1,899 | 1,037 | 2,936 |

  - Genotype runs (WT vs. KO, same tissue/fraction): low MNAR counts (95-235). Most proteins detected in both conditions.
  - Fraction/region runs (cyto vs. synap, CTX vs. HIP): high MNAR counts (141-633). Many proteins genuinely compartment-specific. Biologically expected.
  - HIPcyto_WT_vs_HIPsynap_WT has the most total imputed values (2,936): most divergent comparison in the dataset.

### 2026-03-30 (Claude Code) -- Module 03 IMPUTE Implemented, Tested, and Wired

- **bin/impute.py implemented (Module 03 IMPUTE process):**
  - MNAR/MAR classification (DEP-style): SINGLE-GROUP proteins → MNAR (absent group imputed with MinProb, sporadic in detected group → MAR). PASSED proteins with any NaN → MAR. Complete proteins → none.
  - MinProb imputation (MNAR): per-sample random draws from Normal(q-th quantile, scale × SD) of observed values. Uses `np.random.default_rng(seed)` (modern numpy Generator API, not global state) for determinism. Parameters: `minprob_quantile: 0.01`, `minprob_scale: 0.3`, `random_seed: 42`.
  - KNN imputation (MAR): sklearn `KNNImputer` on (proteins × samples) matrix directly. Each row = protein, each column = sample; finds k nearest proteins by abundance profile. No transpose needed -- KNNImputer in this orientation does protein-wise KNN. Parameter: `knn_k: 10`.
  - MinProb fills MNAR positions first; KNNImputer then fills remaining NaN (MAR positions only).
  - Single-method mode (`imputation.mode: single`): minprob, knn, or left_censored applied uniformly to all missing values.
  - Two diagnostic plots via Plotly (PNG + HTML):
    1. KDE density curves: observed (blue), MNAR-imputed (red dashed), MAR-imputed (green dotted). Uses `scipy.stats.gaussian_kde` with Scott bandwidth.
    2. Per-sample imputation fraction bar chart, colored by group.
  - 5 output files: imputed_matrix.parquet, imputation_summary.parquet, imputation_summary.csv, imputation_summary.txt, plus 4 plot files (2 plot types × PNG/HTML).

- **modules/local/impute/main.nf created:**
  - 8 named emit channels: `imputed_matrix`, `imputation_summary`, `imputation_summary_csv`, `imputation_summary_txt`, `plot_distributions_png`, `plot_distributions_html`, `plot_fractions_png`, `plot_fractions_html`.
  - Publishes to `{run_id}/normalization/` (same directory as NORMALIZE).
  - Stub block touches all 8 expected files.

- **workflows/prosift.nf updated:**
  - `ch_input` multiMap expanded to 6 branches: `impute_params` added as 6th branch.
  - IMPUTE wired: `NORMALIZE.out.normalized_matrix.join(VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.metadata).join(FILTER_PROTEINS.out.filter_table).join(ch_input.impute_params)` → `ch_impute_input` → `IMPUTE(ch_impute_input)`.
  - Module import added.

- **Standalone smoke test on CTXcyto benchmark:**
  - 8,403 proteins, 958 NaN values → 0 NaN in imputed matrix. All 8 output files produced.
  - 124 MNAR proteins (all SINGLE-GROUP): 372 values imputed with MinProb (all 3 absent-group replicates per protein).
  - 461 MAR proteins (PASSED with sporadic NaN): 586 values imputed with KNN.
  - MNAR imputed values: mean 15.395, max 18.275, all below 25th percentile of observed distribution (21.009). Expected behavior for low-end imputation.
  - MAR imputed values: within the main observed distribution (not low-end cluster). Correct behavior.

- **Determinism confirmed:** Two independent runs with `random_seed: 42` produced identical MD5 hash for imputed_matrix.parquet (`5901db65f5d3a8107c066b353808da92`). Nextflow -resume caching will work correctly.

- **Comprehensive spot-check (13 checks, all passed):**
  1. Protein count preserved (8,403). PASS.
  2. No NaN in imputed matrix. PASS.
  3. Column structure: protein_id + 6 abundance_ + 6 peptide_count_. PASS.
  4. Peptide count columns passed through unmodified. PASS.
  5. MNAR count: 124 proteins (correct -- matches SINGLE-GROUP count from Module 01 filter table). PASS.
  6. MAR count: 461 proteins (correct -- PASSED proteins with NaN after filter). PASS.
  7. MNAR total values: 372 (124 proteins × 3 absent-group replicates). PASS.
  8. MAR total values: 586 (sporadic NaN across 461 PASSED proteins). PASS.
  9. MNAR imputed values cluster at low end: max 18.275 < 25th percentile 21.009. Initial spot-check appeared to fail (max 22.5) because it collected ALL NaN for MNAR proteins including sporadic MAR positions in the detected group. Corrected to collect only truly absent-group positions: max 18.275. PASS (not a bug in impute.py).
  10. MAR imputed values are within main distribution (not low-end). Mean 23.3 vs. observed mean 23.5. PASS.
  11. Protein row order preserved. PASS.
  12. Determinism: identical MD5 on two independent runs. PASS.
  13. All 8 expected output files present. PASS.

- **Stub test on all 12 runs:** All passed. IMPUTE outputs published to `results/{run_id}/normalization/` for all 12 runs.

### 2026-03-30 (Claude Code) -- Module 03 NORMALIZE Implemented, Tested, and Wired

- **bin/normalize.py implemented (Module 03 NORMALIZE process):**
  - Method-aware log2 + normalization dispatcher: `median` (additive shift on log2 scale), `quantile` (rank-based with NaN handling), `vsn` (R vsn2()+predict() via rpy2, NaN positions restored after transform), `none` (log2-only or pass-through).
  - VSN implementation uses `vsn2()` + `predict()` rather than `justvsn()` to handle NaN values correctly. NaN mask saved before R call, restored after.
  - CV computed on back-transformed linear scale (2^log2), sample std (ddof=1). Proteins with <2 observed values in a group get NaN CV.
  - Post-normalization diagnostic plots via `bin/prosift_plot_utils.py`: intensity boxplots, PCA, correlation heatmap (all with `label="Post-Normalization"`).
  - Correlation heatmap color scale fix also applied this session: `zmin` now floored to nearest 0.05 below minimum off-diagonal correlation (prevents compressing the full color range into a tiny span); annotation text rounded to 3dp (prevents "same label, different color" visual inconsistency from 2dp rounding).

- **modules/local/normalize/main.nf created:**
  - 12 named emit channels: `normalized_matrix`, `normalization_summary`, `cv_summary`, `cv_summary_csv`, `pca_results`, `correlation_matrix`, 3 plot pairs (PNG + HTML). Publishes to `{run_id}/normalization/`.
  - Stub block touches all 12 expected output files.

- **workflows/prosift.nf updated:**
  - `ch_input` multiMap expanded from 4 to 5 branches: `normalize_params` added.
  - NORMALIZE wired: `FILTER_PROTEINS.out.matrix.join(VALIDATE_INPUTS.out.metadata).join(ch_input.normalize_params)` → `ch_normalize_input` → `NORMALIZE(ch_normalize_input)`. Runs in parallel with PRENORM_QC (no dependency between them).
  - Module 03 import added.

- **Standalone smoke test on CTXcyto benchmark:**
  - 8,403 proteins, 6 samples, median normalization. All 12 output files produced.
  - CV medians: KO 0.090 (3.2% proteins CV > 50%), WT 0.082 (1.4% proteins CV > 50%).

- **Comprehensive spot-check (10 checks, all passed):**
  - Protein count preserved (8,403 in/out). Column structure correct (protein_id + 6 abundance_ + 6 peptide_count_). NaN positions unchanged. Peptide counts unmodified. All 6 sample medians identical post-normalization (max deviation 3.55e-15, floating-point noise). Log2 values in expected range (11.6-35.2). CV values match manual re-derivation with ddof=1 (apparent mismatch with numpy default ddof=0 was in the spot-check script, not normalize.py). Proteins with 0 observed values correctly get NaN CV. Protein row order preserved. All 12 output files present.

- **Stub test on all 12 runs:** 72 total tasks (6 processes × 12 runs), all passed. NORMALIZE outputs published to `results/{run_id}/normalization/` for all 12 runs.

*Session log entries from 2026-03-20 through 2026-03-27 archived (see Archived Sessions at end of document). All substantive content promoted to Project Documentation.*

---

# 4. Task Queue

## 4.1 Pending Tasks

> **Entry template:**
>
> ### [Task Title]
> - **Assigned to:** [Claude.ai / Claude Code]
> - **Priority:** [High / Medium / Low]
> - **Phase:** [1 / 2 / 3 / 4]
> - **Module:** [Which module this belongs to]
> - **Description:** [What needs to be done]
> - **Context/References:** [Relevant Project Documentation sections, prior session log entries]
> - **Acceptance criteria:** [How to know when it's done]
> - **Dependencies:** [Other tasks or modules that must be complete first]

None currently.

## 4.2 Decisions and Designs for Review

> Implementation choices, design tradeoffs, or prototype outputs produced by Claude Code
> that need user or Claude.ai sign-off before building further.

None currently.

## 4.3 Completed Tasks

*Detailed completed task log (2026-03-20 through 2026-03-30) archived. Summary: data prep scripts, Module 01 (all processes), Module 02, Module 03 (NORMALIZE + IMPUTE), full pipeline test all 12 runs, Module 04 design. See Project Documentation Section 10 (Development Log) for details.*

- Module 04 design spec completed -- 2026-03-30 -- project_documentation/modules/04_differential_abundance.Rmd. 9 design decisions, 5 parameters, 14-column output schema, spot-check plan.
- Module 04 (Differential Abundance) implemented and tested -- 2026-03-30 -- bin/differential_abundance.py, modules/local/differential_abundance/main.nf, wired in workflows/prosift.nf. Full 12-run pipeline test passed. See session log entry 2026-03-30 for details.

---

# 5. Open Questions and Blockers

## 5.1 Blockers

> Issues that are preventing progress on a specific module or task.
>
> **Entry format:** [DATE opened] [Module affected] [Description] [Status/progress]

None currently.

## 5.2 Open Questions

> Questions that need resolution but are not blocking immediate work.
>
> **Entry format:** [DATE opened] [Question] [Status/progress]

- 2026-03-20 -- PubMed co-occurrence normalization approach not yet selected [See Project Documentation Section 4.3]
- 2026-03-20 -- Frontend technology not yet selected (deferred to after Phase 2) [See Project Documentation Section 3.7]
- 2026-03-30 -- New bin/ scripts must have `chmod +x` applied immediately after creation. The `Write` tool creates files without the execute bit; Nextflow calls bin/ scripts directly as executables and will fail with `Permission denied` if the bit is missing. Standalone testing (via `python bin/script.py`) bypasses this requirement and will not catch the issue.
- 2026-03-26 -- ID mapping cache has no schema version. If parsing logic changes (new status added, field renamed), existing cache files will be loaded and served with the old classification without any warning. Adding a `schema_version` field to the cache metadata JSON would protect against this. Low priority now; becomes important before Phase 3 changes to the mapping logic.
- 2026-03-26 -- UniProt cache dir on cluster must be on persistent storage. Currently `{params.outdir}/cache/id_mapping`. Confirm `params.outdir` resolves to a persistent mount (home2 or scratch, not a temp FS) before first cluster run. If outdir is on scratch, the cache may be deleted between submissions, defeating the point.
- 2026-03-26 -- Input usability: current workflow requires 3 preprocessing scripts + samplesheet + per-run params/metadata before pipeline runs. Acceptable for development; not suitable for bench scientists. The usability layer sits entirely outside the pipeline and can be rewritten at any point without architectural changes. Deferred to post-Phase 1. [See also Project Documentation Section 6.5]
- 2026-03-27 -- Cluster setup: `vsn` R package requires `BiocManager::install("vsn")` after conda env creation. Not available via bioconda. Must be done manually on cluster. Similarly, verify `bioconductor-deqms` installs via bioconda; fallback is `BiocManager::install("DEqMS")`.
- 2026-03-27 -- Kaleido (Plotly static PNG export) needs verification on cluster. Requires headless Chromium. Install via `conda install -c conda-forge python-kaleido` in prosift env on mesx.sdsu.edu.
- 2026-03-30 -- rpy2 requires R_HOME (and DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH on macOS) to be set explicitly; without them rpy2 segfaults (exit 139) on macOS even when R is on PATH. Fixed locally via `process.beforeScript` in the conda profile (nextflow.config). Must verify the equivalent is set correctly for the cluster pbs profile before running Module 04 on mesx.sdsu.edu.
- 2026-03-30 -- kaleido/Chrome concurrency cap: `maxForks = 2` added for all plotting processes in conf/base.config (PRENORM_QC, NORMALIZE, IMPUTE, MISSINGNESS_REPORT, DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE). Running 12 Nextflow tasks in parallel on macOS causes Chrome to hang. This is harmless on the cluster (separate nodes per task) but the setting must remain. If more plotting processes are added in future modules, apply `maxForks = 2` to them as well.

## 5.3 Resolved Questions

*Archived. Prior resolved questions (benchmark dataset selection, tools survey, conda path workaround, samplesheet generation, VSN package availability, VSN+NA handling, MinProb reproducibility) all captured in Project Documentation or module specs.*

---

# 6. Integration Points

This section tracks the data contracts between modules -- what each module produces and what downstream modules expect to consume. When a module's interface changes during implementation, update this section immediately and check all affected downstream modules.

[See Project Documentation Section 2.2 for the full pipeline DAG and dependency logic.]

## 6.1 Data Contracts

Each entry defines what a module outputs and which downstream modules consume that output. Add entries here as modules are implemented, using the following template:

> ### [Producer Module] --> [Consumer Module]
> - **Producer:** [Nextflow process or module name]
> - **Consumer:** [Nextflow process or module name]
> - **Format:** [Parquet / CSV / JSON / etc.]
> - **Schema:** [Column names, types, and any constraints]
> - **Notes:** [Edge cases, relevant Project Documentation references]

[See Project Documentation Section 2.2 for the full pipeline DAG and dependency logic.]

No data contracts defined yet. Populate as modules are implemented.

### Module 01 (Input Validation & ID Mapping) --> Module 02 (Pre-Normalization QC/EDA)
- **Producer:** FILTER_PROTEINS, UNIPROT_MAPPING
- **Consumer:** Module 02 Pre-Normalization QC/EDA process(es) (not yet implemented)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.filtered_matrix.parquet` -- post-detection-filter abundance data (~8,500-8,800 proteins x 6 samples per run, plus peptide count columns). Parquet format. Produced by FILTER_PROTEINS.
  - `{run_id}.id_mapping.parquet` -- protein ID mapping table (10 columns: protein_id, uniprot_accession, gene_symbol_mouse, entrez_id_mouse, ensembl_gene_mouse, human_ortholog_symbol [null], human_ortholog_entrez [null], ortholog_mapping_status [null], mapping_status, mapping_notes). One row per protein. Produced by UNIPROT_MAPPING.
- **Join key:** `protein_id` (UniProt accession, first from semicolon-delimited protein group). NOTE: for 6 accession-redirected proteins per run, `protein_id` (input accession) differs from `uniprot_accession` (canonical). Downstream joins must use `protein_id`, not `uniprot_accession`.
- **Also available (diagnostic, not primary):** validation_report_part1.txt, validation_report_part2.txt, validation_report_part3.txt -- published to `results/{run_id}/validation/` but not consumed by downstream processes.
- **NOTE:** `detection_filter_table.csv` was previously diagnostic-only but is now also a primary analytical input for the IMPUTE process (MNAR/MAR classification). See Module 01 -> Module 03 IMPUTE contract below.

### Module 01 (Input Validation & ID Mapping) --> Module 03 NORMALIZE
- **Producer:** FILTER_PROTEINS, VALIDATE_INPUTS
- **Consumer:** NORMALIZE (not yet implemented, Phase 2)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.filtered_matrix.parquet` -- same as Module 02 input. The main analytical spine passes through Module 03, not Module 02 (Module 02 branches off for QC without modifying data).
  - `{run_id}.validated_metadata.parquet` -- sample-to-group assignments.
- **Note:** Module 02's outputs (sample_flags, QC report) are diagnostic and do not feed into Module 03. The data flowing to Module 03 is the same filtered matrix from Module 01.

### Module 01 (Input Validation & ID Mapping) --> Module 03 IMPUTE
- **Producer:** FILTER_PROTEINS
- **Consumer:** IMPUTE (not yet implemented, Phase 2)
- **Primary data artifact:**
  - `{run_id}.detection_filter_table.csv` -- per-protein filter status (PASSED, SINGLE-GROUP, SPARSE, ABSENT) and per-group detection counts. Used for MNAR/MAR classification: SINGLE-GROUP = MNAR, PASSED with missing values = MAR.
- **Note:** This file was previously diagnostic-only. It is now a primary analytical input because the DEP-style mixed imputation classification maps directly to Module 01's filter categories.

### Module 03 NORMALIZE --> Module 03 IMPUTE
- **Producer:** NORMALIZE (functional, 2026-03-30)
- **Consumer:** IMPUTE (functional, 2026-03-30)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.normalized_matrix.parquet` -- approximately log2-scale, normalized abundance data. Same shape as filtered matrix. Peptide count columns passed through unchanged. Missing values remain NaN.
- **Data scale:** Always approximately log2, regardless of normalization method.
- **Join key:** `protein_id`.

### Module 03 IMPUTE --> Module 04 (Differential Abundance)
- **Producer:** IMPUTE (functional, 2026-03-30)
- **Consumer:** DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE (functional, 2026-03-30)
- **Primary data artifacts:**
  - `{run_id}.imputed_matrix.parquet` -- fully imputed, approximately log2-scale, normalized. No NaN values. Contains abundance columns (`abundance_{sample_id}`) and peptide count columns (`peptide_count_{sample_id}`, int64, zeros at imputed positions). This is the analysis-ready matrix.
- **Additional inputs from Module 01:**
  - `{run_id}.validated_metadata.parquet` -- sample-to-group assignments (from VALIDATE_INPUTS)
  - `{run_id}.id_mapping.parquet` -- protein IDs and gene symbols (from UNIPROT_MAPPING)
- **Join key:** `protein_id`.

### Module 04 (Differential Abundance) --> Module 05 (Enrichment)
- **Producer:** DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE (functional, 2026-03-30)
- **Consumer:** Module 05 Enrichment (not yet designed)
- **Primary data artifact:**
  - `{run_id}.diff_abundance_results.parquet` -- 14-column results table (see Module 04 spec Section 4.5). One row per protein per contrast. All proteins included regardless of significance.
- **Consumer usage:** GSEA uses the full ranked list (rank by signed -log10 p-value or logFC). ORA filters to significant proteins (significant == True).
- **Join key:** `protein_id`.

### Module 04 (Differential Abundance) --> Module 06 (Database Queries)
- **Producer:** DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE (functional, 2026-03-30)
- **Consumer:** Module 06 Database Queries (not yet designed)
- **Primary data artifact:**
  - `{run_id}.diff_abundance_results.parquet` -- same file as Module 05 input.
- **Consumer usage:** Filters to significant proteins, or uses all proteins if `databases.query_scope = "all"`.
- **Join key:** `protein_id`.

### Module 03 --> QC Report Assembly
- **Producer:** NORMALIZE and IMPUTE (both functional, 2026-03-30)
- **Consumer:** QC Report Assembly process (not yet implemented, Phase 2)
- **Diagnostic artifacts from NORMALIZE:**
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_boxplots` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_pca_scatter` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_correlation_heatmap` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_pca_results.parquet`
  - `{run_id}.postnorm_correlation_matrix.parquet`
  - `{run_id}.cv_summary.parquet` + `.csv`
  - `{run_id}.normalization_summary.txt`
- **Diagnostic artifacts from IMPUTE:**
  - `{run_id}.imputation_distributions` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.imputation_fractions` (PNG + HTML)
  - `{run_id}.imputation_summary.parquet` + `.csv`
  - `{run_id}.imputation_summary.txt`
- **Note:** Post-normalization plots mirror Module 02's pre-normalization plots for before/after comparison.

---

# 7. File System Map

## 7.1 Directory Structure

**Last updated by Claude Code:** 2026-03-30 (Module 04 implementation complete)

```
ProSIFT/
│
├── .gitignore
├── CLAUDE.md                                # Project-level Claude Code instructions
├── environment.yml                          # Conda environment spec (Python 3.12, all Phase 1+ deps)
├── main.nf                                  # Nextflow entry point
├── nextflow.config                          # Pipeline config (params, profiles: local/pbs/conda/singularity)
│
├── conf/
│   └── base.config                          # Default resource allocations per process
│
├── workflows/
│   └── prosift.nf                           # Top-level PROSIFT workflow (all Phase 1 processes wired; multiMap cleanup 2026-03-27)
│
├── modules/
│   └── local/
│       ├── validate_inputs/main.nf          # Process: VALIDATE_INPUTS (functional)
│       ├── filter_proteins/main.nf          # Process: FILTER_PROTEINS (functional)
│       ├── uniprot_mapping/main.nf          # Process: UNIPROT_MAPPING (functional)
│       ├── prenorm_qc/main.nf              # Process: PRENORM_QC (functional)
│       ├── missingness_report/main.nf       # Process: MISSINGNESS_REPORT (functional)
│       ├── normalize/main.nf               # Process: NORMALIZE (functional, 2026-03-30)
│       ├── impute/main.nf                  # Process: IMPUTE (functional, 2026-03-30)
│       └── differential_abundance/main.nf  # Process: DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE (functional, 2026-03-30)
│
├── bin/                                     # Python scripts called from Nextflow processes (executable, shebangs set)
│   ├── validate_inputs.py                   # Processes 4.1-4.3: matrix/metadata/cross-validation
│   ├── filter_proteins.py                   # Process 4.4: detection filter + missingness overview
│   ├── uniprot_mapping.py                   # Processes 4.5-4.9: UniProt bulk ID mapping + cache
│   ├── prenorm_qc.py                       # Module 02: pre-normalization QC/EDA (functional)
│   ├── missingness_report.py               # Process 4.10: missingness visualizations (functional, 2026-03-27)
│   ├── prosift_plot_utils.py               # Shared plotting utilities for Modules 02-03 (functional, 2026-03-27)
│   ├── normalize.py                         # Module 03 NORMALIZE: log2 + normalization + diagnostics + CV (functional, 2026-03-30)
│   ├── impute.py                            # Module 03 IMPUTE: MNAR/MAR classification + imputation + diagnostics (functional, 2026-03-30)
│   └── differential_abundance.py            # Module 04: limma + DEqMS via rpy2; volcano + MA plots; 14-column results (functional, 2026-03-30)
│
├── project_documentation/
│   ├── ProSIFT_project_documentation.Rmd    # Architectural reference (Project Documentation)
│   ├── ProSIFT_handoff.Rmd                  # THIS FILE - operational coordination
│   ├── ProSIFT_handoff.html                 # Rendered handoff
│   ├── figures/
│   │   ├── prosift_pipeline_dag.svg         # Pipeline DAG diagram
│   │   └── prosift_input_validation_workflow.svg
│   └── modules/
│       ├── 01_input_validation.Rmd          # Module 01: Input Validation & ID Mapping (design complete, implemented)
│       ├── 02_qc_eda.Rmd                    # Module 02: Pre-Normalization QC/EDA (design complete, implemented)
│       ├── 03_normalization_imputation.Rmd  # Module 03: Normalization & Imputation (design complete, implemented, tested)
│       ├── 03b_qc_report_assembly.Rmd       # QC Report Assembly checkpoint (stub; sits after Module 03, before Module 04)
│       ├── 04_differential_abundance.Rmd    # Module 04: Differential Abundance (design complete 2026-03-30)
│       ├── 05_enrichment.Rmd                # Module 05: ORA/GSEA Enrichment (stub)
│       ├── 06_database_queries.Rmd          # Module 06: Database Queries (stub)
│       ├── 07_results_assembly.Rmd          # Module 07: Results Assembly / SQLite (stub)
│       └── 08_frontend.Rmd                  # Module 08: Interactive Frontend (stub)
│
├── CTX_synaptosome_project_data/
│   └── Filtered Proteins-Table 1.csv        # Raw DIA-NN output (source data, not committed)
│
├── scripts/                                 # Data preparation scripts (not part of pipeline)
│   ├── prepare_ctx_data.py                  # CTX-specific: raw DIA-NN -> master ProSIFT files
│   ├── generate_run_config.py               # Interactive: master files -> run_config.yml
│   └── prepare_prosift_input.py             # run_config.yml -> per-run input directories + samplesheet.csv
│
├── prosift_inputs/                          # Pre-pipeline inputs (generated by data prep scripts)
│   ├── CTX_synaptosome_abundance.csv        # Master abundance (8,948 proteins x 49 cols; shared by all runs)
│   ├── CTX_synaptosome_metadata.csv         # Master metadata (24 samples)
│   ├── CTX_synaptosome_protein_reference.csv # Protein reference (gene symbols, descriptions)
│   ├── run_config.yml                       # 12 run definitions (generated by generate_run_config.py)
│   ├── samplesheet.csv                      # Nextflow input: one row per run (run_id, abundance, metadata, params)
│   ├── CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO/           # Per-run directories (12 total, 2 files each)
│   │   ├── CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO_metadata.csv   # sample_id + group column for this run's 6 samples
│   │   └── CTXcyto_WT_vs_CTXcyto_KO_params.yml     # Run params; abundance_matrix -> ../CTX_synaptosome_abundance.csv
│   └── [11 more run directories, same structure]
│
├── work/                                    # Nextflow work directory (not committed; auto-generated on run)
│
└── results/                                 # Pipeline outputs (not committed; populated on run)
    ├── cache/
    │   └── id_mapping/                      # UniProt API response cache (persists across runs; key = MD5 hash)
    ├── pipeline_info/                       # Nextflow execution trace, timeline, report
    └── {run_id}/
        ├── validation/                      # Module 01 outputs per run
        │   ├── {run_id}.filtered_matrix.parquet        # Proteins passing detection filter (input to Modules 02, 03)
        │   ├── {run_id}.detection_filter_table.csv     # Per-protein filter status and detection counts
        │   ├── {run_id}.id_mapping.parquet             # UniProt mapping table (10 cols; input to all downstream modules)
        │   ├── {run_id}.validation_report_part1.txt    # Processes 4.1-4.3 report
        │   ├── {run_id}.validation_report_part2.txt    # Process 4.4 report (filter summary + missingness)
        │   ├── {run_id}.validation_report_part3.txt    # Processes 4.5-4.9 report (mapping summary + coverage)
        │   ├── {run_id}.missingness_report.html        # Process 4.10: interactive missingness report (HTML with 4 Plotly plots)
        │   └── {run_id}.missingness_plots/             # Process 4.10: static PNGs (4 files: filter_categories, per_sample_detection, missingness_heatmap, missingness_histogram)
        ├── qc/                              # Module 02 outputs per run (functional)
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_summary.parquet          # Per-sample metrics
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_summary.csv
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_flags.parquet             # Per-sample outlier flags
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_flags.csv
        │   ├── {run_id}.correlation_matrix.parquet       # Sample-by-sample Pearson correlation
        │   ├── {run_id}.pca_results.parquet              # PCA coordinates + variance explained
        │   ├── {run_id}.prenorm_qc_report.html           # Consolidated QC report with all plots
        │   ├── {run_id}.sample_intensity_summary.png/.html
        │   ├── {run_id}.intensity_boxplots.png/.html
        │   ├── {run_id}.qq_plot.png/.html
        │   ├── {run_id}.pca_scatter.png/.html
        │   └── {run_id}.correlation_heatmap.png/.html
        └── normalization/                     # Module 03 NORMALIZE + IMPUTE outputs per run (both functional, full pipeline tested 2026-03-30)
            ├── {run_id}.normalized_matrix.parquet        # Normalized data (approx log2; input to IMPUTE)
            ├── {run_id}.normalization_summary.txt        # Method used, log2 applied, warnings
            ├── {run_id}.cv_summary.parquet               # Per-protein per-group CV (linear scale)
            ├── {run_id}.cv_summary.csv
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_pca_results.parquet
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_correlation_matrix.parquet
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_boxplots.png/.html
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_pca_scatter.png/.html
            ├── {run_id}.postnorm_correlation_heatmap.png/.html
            ├── {run_id}.imputed_matrix.parquet           # Fully imputed data (no NaN; input to Module 04)
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_summary.parquet       # Per-protein imputation class and counts
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_summary.csv
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_summary.txt
            ├── {run_id}.imputation_distributions.png/.html
            └── {run_id}.imputation_fractions.png/.html
        └── differential_abundance/             # Module 04 outputs per run (functional, full pipeline tested 2026-03-30)
            ├── {run_id}.diff_abundance_results.parquet           # Full results table (14 cols, all proteins)
            ├── {run_id}.diff_abundance_results.csv
            ├── {run_id}.diff_abundance_summary.txt
            ├── {run_id}.{contrast}.volcano_plot.png/.html        # One pair per contrast
            └── {run_id}.{contrast}.ma_plot.png/.html             # One pair per contrast
```

## 7.2 Scripts Reference

| Script | Module | Description | Last Updated |
|---|---|---|---|
| scripts/prepare_ctx_data.py | Data prep | Converts raw DIA-NN CTX output to master ProSIFT-format files | 2026-03-25 |
| scripts/generate_run_config.py | Data prep | Interactive tool: define pairwise runs from master files, outputs run_config.yml | 2026-03-25 |
| scripts/prepare_prosift_input.py | Data prep | Splits master files into per-run input directories using run_config.yml; generates samplesheet.csv | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/validate_inputs.py | Module 01 | Processes 4.1-4.3: validates abundance matrix, metadata, cross-validates samples; outputs validated Parquets + report part 1 | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/filter_proteins.py | Module 01 | Process 4.4: detection filter; classifies proteins PASSED/SINGLE-GROUP/SPARSE/ABSENT; outputs filtered Parquet, filter table, report part 2 | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/uniprot_mapping.py | Module 01 | Processes 4.5-4.9: UniProt bulk ID mapping via paginated JSON results endpoint; MD5-keyed file cache; ortholog columns null (Phase 3); outputs id_mapping.parquet + report part 3 | 2026-03-26 |
| bin/missingness_report.py | Module 01 | Process 4.10: 4 missingness plots (filter categories, per-sample detection, heatmap, histogram); HTML report + static PNGs via kaleido | 2026-03-27 |
| bin/prenorm_qc.py | Module 02 | Pre-normalization QC/EDA: per-sample summaries, box plots, Q-Q plot, PCA, correlation heatmap, outlier flagging; outputs 4 data files + 5 plot types + HTML report. Imports shared plot functions from prosift_plot_utils.py. | 2026-03-27 |
| bin/prosift_plot_utils.py | Shared | Shared plotting utilities for Modules 02 and 03: plot_intensity_boxplots, compute_and_plot_pca, compute_and_plot_correlation, make_color_map, save_plot. label and note parameters allow phase-specific titles and annotations. | 2026-03-27 |
| bin/normalize.py | Module 03 | NORMALIZE process: method-aware log2 + median/quantile/VSN/none + post-norm diagnostics (via prosift_plot_utils.py) + CV on back-transformed linear scale. VSN uses vsn2()+predict() via rpy2. | 2026-03-30 |
| bin/impute.py | Module 03 | IMPUTE process: DEP-style MNAR/MAR classification from detection_filter_table.csv; MinProb (np.random.default_rng, per-sample low-end draws) for MNAR; KNN (sklearn KNNImputer, protein-wise, no transpose) for MAR; left_censored available in single mode. 2 diagnostic plot types (KDE distributions, imputation fraction bar chart). 8 output files. | 2026-03-30 |
| bin/differential_abundance.py | Module 04 | DIFFERENTIAL_ABUNDANCE process: contrast parsing + validation, peptide count summarization (min of nonzero), limma + DEqMS via rpy2 (localconverter API), 14-column results table, volcano + MA plots per contrast (PNG + HTML). R_HOME required for rpy2 on macOS. | 2026-03-30 |

## 7.3 File System Update Instructions

When creating, moving, or deleting files:

1. Update the directory tree above
2. Update the "Last updated" date
3. Add notes for new directories or significant files
4. Update Scripts Reference table if new scripts created
5. Check if new files need GitHub inclusion/exclusion (see Sections 8.4-8.5)

---

# 8. Computational Environment

## 8.1 Platform

**Local machine:** macOS (development, documentation, git)

**Cluster:** mesx.sdsu.edu (pipeline execution)

*Surveyed via SSH 2026-03-25. All entries below are confirmed unless noted.*

- SSH access: `ssh rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu` (key-based auth, no password)
- OS: Oracle Linux Server 8.10, x86_64
- Home directory: `/home2/rhastings` (2.5 TB available)
- Scratch directory: `/usr/scratch2/userdata2/rhastings` (11 TB available)
- Conda: miniconda3 25.1.1 at `/home2/rhastings/miniconda3`; Python 3.12.9 in base
- Existing conda envs: `base`, `classes`, `ml_hop` -- no `prosift` env yet
- Job scheduler: **Torque 6** at `/usr/local/torque6/bin` (on PATH); `pbspro` Nextflow executor is correct
- Java: not installed system-wide; will be installed via `openjdk=17` in prosift conda env
- Nextflow: not installed; will be installed via conda
- R: not installed; will be installed via conda (r-base>=4.3)
- Singularity/Apptainer: not installed; deferred to later phase
- Git: not installed on cluster (by design -- rsync workflow, see Section 8.2)

## 8.2 Development Workflow: Local + Cluster via rsync

**Decision (2026-03-23):** Code is developed and documented locally. Files are synced to the cluster via `rsync` for execution. Git is used only for intentional milestones, not for shuttling changes between machines.

**Why this approach:** The cluster lacks git. Installing it is possible (via conda) but using git to sync between local and cluster would clutter the commit history with small "fix path" / "increase memory" commits during iterative debugging. rsync provides instant, low-friction syncing over SSH with no version control overhead. Git commits are reserved for meaningful checkpoints ("QC module working", "imputation implemented").

**Sync local to cluster (push code for execution):**

```bash
rsync -avz --exclude='.git' --exclude='data/' --exclude='results/' \
  --exclude='.nextflow/' --exclude='work/' --exclude='.DS_Store' \
  "/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Blanco-Suárez Lab/ProSIFT/" \
  rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu:~/ProSIFT/
```

**Sync cluster to local (pull back bug fixes or results):**

```bash
# Pull back code changes (e.g., after fixing a bug on the cluster)
rsync -avz rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu:~/ProSIFT/workflow/ \
  "/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Blanco-Suárez Lab/ProSIFT/workflow/"

# Pull back results (when needed for local inspection)
rsync -avz rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu:~/ProSIFT/results/ \
  "/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Blanco-Suárez Lab/ProSIFT/results/"
```

**What does NOT get synced to the cluster:**

- `.git/` directory (git is local only)
- `data/` and `results/` (data lives on the cluster; results are pulled back selectively)
- `.nextflow/` and `work/` (Nextflow work directories are cluster-local)
- Project documentation (not needed for pipeline execution)

**Instructions for Claude Code sessions:**

- Use `rsync` commands above to sync code to/from the cluster. Do not attempt to use git on the cluster.
- Always sync local → cluster before asking the user to run on the cluster.
- After debugging on the cluster, sync cluster → local to capture fixes before committing.
- Do not sync without explicit user approval -- rsync can overwrite files in either direction.

**When to graduate to git-on-cluster:** If iterative cluster debugging becomes frequent enough that rsync feels limiting (e.g., needing to track what changed on the cluster between runs), install git via conda on the cluster and switch to a `dev` branch workflow with squash merges to `main`.

## 8.3 Environment Setup

**Development environment:** Conda -- `environment.yml` finalized 2026-03-25. Pin strategy: Python, pandas, numpy, scipy, rpy2, r-base pinned to major version ranges; other packages unpinned. Python=3.12 to match cluster base.

```bash
# Create environment (first time)
conda env create -f environment.yml

# Update existing environment
conda env update -f environment.yml --prune

# Activate before running any scripts
conda activate prosift
```

**Key dependencies (finalized -- see environment.yml for exact specs):**

| Package | Purpose | Phase |
|---|---|---|
| python=3.12 | Runtime | 1+ |
| openjdk=17 | Required by Nextflow | 1+ |
| nextflow | Pipeline orchestration | 1+ |
| pandas, numpy, pyarrow, pyyaml | Data handling, Parquet intermediates | 1+ |
| requests, tqdm | UniProt API + progress | 1+ |
| r-base, rpy2, bioconductor-limma, bioconductor-deqms | Statistical analysis | 2 |
| scipy, scikit-learn | Statistics, imputation | 2 |
| gseapy | ORA/GSEA enrichment | 2 |
| matplotlib, seaborn, plotly | QC/EDA visualization | 2 |
| pytest, pytest-cov | Testing | 1+ |

**[FOLLOW-UP NEEDED]** Verify `bioconductor-deqms` installs via bioconda. If not available, fallback is `BiocManager::install("DEqMS")` from within R after env creation.

**Production environment:** Singularity container (.sif) -- deferred. [See Project Documentation Section 5.2]

**Cluster setup sequence (first time):**

```bash
# 1. Sync project to cluster
rsync -avz --exclude='.git' --exclude='prosift_inputs/' \
  --exclude='.nextflow/' --exclude='work/' --exclude='.DS_Store' \
  "/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Blanco-Suárez Lab/ProSIFT/" \
  rhastings@mesx.sdsu.edu:~/ProSIFT/

# 2. On cluster: create prosift environment
conda env create -f ~/ProSIFT/environment.yml

# 3. Verify Nextflow can find Java
conda activate prosift && nextflow -version
```

## 8.4 GitHub Repository

**Remote:** `git@github.com:ReitheHeroine/ProSIFT.git` (SSH)

**Branch strategy:** `main` is the default and primary branch. Feature branches may be used as the project grows.

**Common operations:**

```bash
# Check status before committing
git status

# Stage specific files (preferred over git add -A)
git add <file1> <file2>

# Commit with a descriptive message
git commit -m "Short summary of what changed and why"

# Push to remote
git push origin main

# Pull latest changes (e.g., after editing on another machine or via GitHub)
git pull origin main
```

**What gets committed:**

- All code (Nextflow workflows, scripts, modules)
- Configuration files (nextflow.config, default params.yml)
- Documentation (project_documentation/*.Rmd, *.svg)
- Environment definitions (environment.yml, Singularity.def)
- Pipeline entry point (main.nf)

**What does NOT get committed** (handled by .gitignore):

- Data files (data/, results/)
- Container images (*.sif)
- Nextflow work directory (.nextflow/, work/)
- Cache files (.cache/)
- System/IDE files (.DS_Store, .vscode/, __pycache__/)

**Instructions for Claude Code sessions:**

- Do not push to remote without explicit user approval
- Stage files by name, not with `git add -A`
- Do not commit files that may contain sensitive data (.env, API keys, credentials)
- When committing, write messages that describe the *why*, not just the *what*
- Git commits are for meaningful milestones only (see Section 8.2) -- do not commit to shuttle small fixes between machines

## 8.5 Repository Configuration (.gitignore)

The .gitignore file is maintained at the project root. Update it when new file types or directories need to be excluded. Current contents reflect the exclusions listed above in Section 8.4.

---

# 9. Code and Style Preferences

## 9.1 Code Header Format

```python
# title: script_name.py
# project: ProSIFT
# author: Reina Hastings
# contact: reinahastings13@gmail.com
# date created: YYYY-MM-DD
# last modified: YYYY-MM-DD
#
# purpose:
#   [Description of what the script does]
#
# inputs:
#   - [Input files/parameters]
#
# outputs:
#   - [Output files/locations]
#
# usage example:
#   python script_name.py --input data.csv --output results/
```

## 9.2 Code Style Conventions

- Use single quotes (`'`) instead of double quotes (`"`) where language allows
- Include argument parsing with `argparse` (Python) or `getopts`/`getopt` (Bash) for script versatility
- Include usage messages so scripts are easy to use by anyone
- Include a copy/paste-ready command in the header's usage example section
- Comment in a visually organized way:
  - Use section headers to delineate major parts of the script
  - Number steps in multi-step algorithms
  - Explain the *why*, not just the *what*, for non-obvious logic
- Write detailed inline comments for complex operations or domain-specific logic

---

# Archived Sessions

[Old session log entries moved here after their content has been captured in the Project Documentation. Kept for reference but not expected to be read routinely.]

**Archived 2026-03-30:** Session log entries from 2026-03-20 through 2026-03-27 (18 entries covering: project inception, DAG design, Nextflow/Snakemake switch, data preparation scripts, cluster survey, environment finalization, Module 01 implementation, Module 01 review and architecture decisions, Module 02 architecture rescoping, Module 02 implementation, Module 03 normalization design, Module 03 imputation design, Module 03 recombination, shared plot utilities creation). All substantive content promoted to Project Documentation Sections 9 (Decision Register) and 10 (Development Log).

**Archived 2026-03-30:** Completed tasks log (36 entries, 2026-03-25 through 2026-03-30) covering data prep, Module 01, Module 02, Module 03, and full pipeline test. Summary in Section 4.3; details in Project Documentation Section 10.

**Archived 2026-03-30:** Resolved questions (benchmark dataset, tools survey, conda path, samplesheet generation, VSN package, VSN+NA, MinProb reproducibility). All captured in Project Documentation or module specs.
